rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees within annex agree blocks.
Ensure type consistency between assumed and actual data types in AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should use valid feature names and avoid implementation-specific references.
Assign statements in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared variables.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
Boolean comparisons should only use exact Boolean expressions when the port is an AGREE bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Use helper declarations for complex boolean expressions like range checks.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type contracts.
Do not reference opaque Boolean or enumeration data as integers in range checks.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens
